defaultOwner: NovaPharm Corporate Communications with Quality and Regulatory review

claims.statement: Oncology and specialist medicines are strategic areas for B2B opportunity assessment.
claims.source: Owner-supplied business plan, treated as future strategy rather than current operation

claims.statement: No oncology medicine is presented as approved, stocked or available from NovaPharm.
claims.source: Public claims boundary and absence of verified product authorisation evidence
claims.expiryTrigger: Verified authorisation and approved product publication

claims.statement: Regulated wholesale activity will begin only after the required authorisations are granted.
The Oncology Supply Continuity Architecture is a proposed governance model connecting product, evidence, source, condition, demand and decision.
NovaPharm does not claim to own a cold chain, laboratory or manufacturing site.
claims.source: Business-plan evidence gate and current public claims register
claims.expiryTrigger: Verified asset ownership and approved publication

claims.statement: Public keyword and optional semantic retrieval process queries in the browser without external AI inference.
The public evidence assistant does not log query or answer text.
claims.source: src/ai/privacy-guard.mjs and absence of network analytics in the AI controller

claims.statement: Portal and private business records are excluded from the public knowledge index.
claims.source: scripts/build-ai-knowledge-index.mjs allowlist and generated source manifest

claims.statement: The protected internal AI gateway defaults to provider none.
AI demand forecasting remains planned and requires governed data, validation and human oversight.
Blockchain is one possible future traceability architecture and is not deployed or proof of source-data correctness.
